channel.title: Agentic Systems on Sange Mehrab
channel.description: Recent content in Agentic Systems on Sange Mehrab

item.title: Karpathy autoresearch
item.description:
Karpathy Autoresearch Explained

Introduction

To build this section, I treated autoresearch as something that has to be reconstructed from public engineering traces rather than from a single formal paper: I checked the official repo, GitHub Discussions session reports, launch and result posts, the nanochat leaderboard notes, and the March 20, 2026 interview, then followed how the idea evolved from a simple “remove yourself as the bottleneck” workflow into a repeatable loop where an autoregressive coding agent proposes edits, a fixed evaluator runs real experiments, and only measured improvements are kept. The public record shows this evolution in practical terms: single-GPU overnight runs improved val_bpb from 0.997900 to 0.969686 in 126 experiments on an H100, and those smaller depth-12 findings later transferred to larger depth-24 nanochat runs, reducing the “time to GPT-2” leaderboard entry from 2.02 hours to 1.80 hours, with a later entry at 1.65 hours. Because I did not find a Karpathy-authored theorem-style paper for the method, the section below explains autoresearch by piecing together its terminology, intuition, formal loop, comparisons, implementations, evidence, and limitations from those public sources.
